MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT GRANTED. BURDEN SHIFTED BY JOHNSON DECLARANT. NO TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. (PB)
OFF CALENDAR, DISMISSAL.
OFF CALENDAR, DISMISSAL.
MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT GRANT BURDEN SHIFTED ON FACTUALLY DEVOID RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES. (PB)
OFF CALENDAR, DEFT DISMISSED.
CONTINUED TO APRIL 30, 2002 TO BE HEARD IN DEPT. 301.
FDCC MSJ: GRANT. No triable issue of fact re exposure. Decedent testimony that he could not remember any specific sites where dinwiddie was general contractor shifts the burden. (CW)
Embarcadero Center MSJ: GRANT. No triable issue of fact re exposure. Plaintiffs special interrogatory response No 4 shifts the burden. (CW)

General Cable MSJ: GRANT. No triable issue of fact re exposure. Plaintiffs' special interrogatory response that it can not identify any specific incident of exposure to Defendant General Cable's asbestos-containing product shifts the burden. (CW)

Kelly-Moore Paint MSJ: DENY. Kelly-Moore failed to shift burden. Standard discovery responses insufficient to shift burden because not focused on eliciting missing information specific to Kelly-Moore. See Schieding v Dinwiddie Construction Company (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 64. Decedent's deposition testimony not sufficient because not asked specifically about Kelly-Moore. See Schieding v Dinwiddie Construction Company (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 64. Ps deposition testimony not sufficient to shift burden because Kelly-Moore failed to establish Ps would have personal knowledge of decedent's working conditions. See Villa v. McFerren (1995) 35 Cal.App.4th 733, 749. (CW)

FP Lathrop MSJ/SA: GRANT No triable issue of fact re exposure. Special Interrogatory Responses shift the burden. 437c(h) continuance DENIED. Counsel's declaration is does not meet requirements of 437c(h). (CW)
OFF CALENDAR, DISMISSAL.
OFF CALENDAR, DISMISSAL.
OFF CALENDAR, DISMISSAL.
MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT GRANTED. BURDEN SHIFTED. NO TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT CREATED BY DEPOSITION TESTIMONY. (PB)
